description: Traefik Ingress Controller via Prometheus Metrics dashboard @ https://github.com/GustavoJST/kubernetes-traefik-ingress-nextgen

panels.description: This is the total number of requests made in this period (top-right period selected)
panels.title: Requests Total (period)

panels.description: The number of HTTP requests made in the last 2 minutes.
panels.title: Reqs (2m)

panels.description:
Percentage of successful requests in the last 2 minutes.

NOTE: Ignoring 404s in this metric, since a  404 is a normal response for errant requests

targets.expr:
sum(rate(traefik_router_requests_total{code!~"[4-5].*", host=~"$host", router=~"$router", pod=~"$pod"}[2m])) / 
sum(rate(traefik_router_requests_total{code!~"404", host=~"$host", router=~"$router", pod=~"$pod"}[2m]))

panels.title: % Success (2m)

panels.description: The number of successful requests in the last 2 minutes.  Successful being 1xx or 2xx by the standard HTTP definition.
panels.title: HTTP 1/2xx (2m)

panels.description: The number of 3xx requests in the 2 minutes.
panels.title: HTTP 3xx (2m)

panels.description: This is the number of 4xx requests in the last 2 minutes.
panels.title: HTTP 4xx (2m)

panels.description: This is the number of 5xx requests in the last 2 minutes.
panels.title: HTTP 5xx (2m)

panels.description:
The request volume of traffic coming to the Traefik pods, measured in requests/second.

The results shown are aggregated across all selected Traefik routers. However, the graph can also be filtered to display specific routers by using the variables at the top of the dashboard.

For a more detailed view broken down by router, open the collapsed tab at the bottom. This view is hidden by default because it is CPU-intensive.
panels.title: Request Volume

panels.description:
The total number of HTTP requests made within each period.

The results shown are aggregated across all selected Traefik routers. However, the graph can also be filtered to display specific routers by using the variables at the top of the dashboard.
panels.title: Total HTTP Requests

panels.description:
The request sucess rate whitin each period.

The results shown are aggregated across all selected Traefik routers. However, the graph can also be filtered to display specific routers by using the variables at the top of the dashboard.

For a more detailed view broken down by router, open the collapsed tab at the bottom. This view is hidden by default because it is CPU-intensive.

targets.expr:
sum(rate(traefik_router_requests_total{code!~"[4-5].*", host=~"$host", router=~"$router", pod=~"$pod"}[$__rate_interval])) / 
sum(rate(traefik_router_requests_total{code!~"404", host=~"$host", router=~"$router", pod=~"$pod"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Request Sucess Rate

panels.description:
The breakdown of the various HTTP status codes of the requests handled within this period that matches the variables chosen above.

This chart helps notice and dive into which service is having failures and of what kind.

The results shown are aggregated across all selected Traefik routers. However, the graph can also be filtered to display specific routers by using the variables at the top of the dashboard.
panels.title: HTTP Status Codes

panels.description:
Ingress latency percentiles, where:

P99 = 99% of requests have a latency equal to or lower than the displayed value.

P90 = 90% of requests have a latency equal to or lower than the displayed value.

P50 = 50% of requests have a latency equal to or lower than the displayed value.

The results shown are aggregated across all selected Traefik routers. However, the graph can also be filtered to display specific routers by using the variables at the top of the dashboard.

For a more detailed latency view broken down by router, open the collapsed tab at the bottom. This view is hidden by default because it is CPU-intensive.
panels.title: Latency (Percentiles)

panels.description:
This graph helps assess and ensure compliance with SLA requirements related to the response time of our services.

The results shown are aggregated across all selected Traefik routers. However, the graph can also be filtered to display specific routers by using the variables at the top of the dashboard.
panels.title: Latency Heatmap

panels.description:
NOTE: This does not work per host/route. You can only filter by Traefik pods.

This is the number of opened connections by Traefik.
panels.title: Open Connections (Controller Pod)

panels.description: Information about expiration dates for certificates used in Ingresses.
panels.title: Certificate Expiry

panels.description:
Ingress latency percentiles, where:

P99 = 99% of requests have a latency equal to or lower than the displayed value.

P90 = 90% of requests have a latency equal to or lower than the displayed value.

P50 = 50% of requests have a latency equal to or lower than the displayed value.

The graph can filtered to display specific routes by using the variables at the top of the dashboard.
panels.title: Latency per Router (Percentiles)

targets.expr:
sum(rate(traefik_router_requests_total{code!~"[4-5].*", host=~"$host", router=~"$router", pod=~"$pod"}[$__rate_interval])) by (router) / 
sum(rate(traefik_router_requests_total{code!~"404", host=~"$host", router=~"$router", pod=~"$pod"}[$__rate_interval])) by (router)

panels.description:
The request volume of traffic coming to the Traefik pods, measured in requests/second.

The results shown are aggregated across all selected Traefik routers. However, the graph can also be filtered to display specific routers by using the variables at the top of the dashboard.

For a more detailed view broken down by router, open the collapsed tab at the bottom. This view is hidden by default because it is CPU-intensive.
panels.title: Request Volume

title: Kubernetes Traefik Ingress NextGen on Prometheus
